source: All-In Podcast — Trump transition, SEC/crypto regulation, xAI Colossus, AI scaling laws, MicroStrategy, defense tech
summary: Nvidia was saved from a difficult 6-month period (Blackwell delay) by Elon's Colossus build, which caused competitors to rush-buy H100 Hoppers
summary_extended: Baker argues that Elon Musk's rapid construction of the Colossus supercluster (100,000 H100 GPUs) served as an inadvertent lifeline for Nvidia during a potentially difficult six-month period caused by Blackwell delays. When Colossus demonstrated that a 100K coherent GPU cluster was achievable — contradicting skepticism from engineers at Meta and Google who believed it was impossible — other hyperscalers and AI labs that had been waiting for Blackwell instead rushed to purchase available Hopper (H100) inventory to build their own large clusters. This demand surge filled the revenue gap that Nvidia would have otherwise faced during the Blackwell transition period. Baker presents this as his own interpretive thesis rather than established fact, framing it as a contingent but plausible reading of the supply/demand dynamics in the GPU market during late 2024. The investment implication is retrospective: Nvidia's revenue continuity through the Blackwell delay was structurally supported by this unexpected demand catalyst, reinforcing the view that Nvidia's dominance in AI compute is resilient even during product transition gaps.
detail: When Blackwell was delayed and engineers at Meta/Google said 100K coherent GPUs was impossible, companies that had been waiting for Blackwell rushed to buy Hoppers to attempt their own large clusters. This created a demand surge that bridged Nvidia's Blackwell gap.
confidence_evidence: Baker states this as his interpretation of events: 'you can argue that Elon doing that kind of saved Nvidia from a tough six-month period'
quote_fragment: Elon doing that kind of saved Nvidia from a tough six-month period when Blackwell was delayed

ticker_audit.reasoning: The thesis is specifically about how Nvidia was protected from a revenue gap during the Blackwell delay by a surge in Hopper demand triggered by Colossus. Nvidia is the direct subject of this thesis.
